response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, accomplished senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems experience, but he is a poor match for this specific role. The hard minimum of 8 years of C++ is far from met — professional C++ appears limited to a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments plus a thesis project, with most systems work in D instead. His cloud experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) is application-level rather than building large-scale compute infrastructure, and while he mentors extensively through workshops and open source, he has not held a formal technical-leadership role owning cross-team strategy, priorities, and delivery.
strengths: Strong low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Proven mentor and knowledge-sharer: multi-day courses, workshops, conference talks, and maintained open-source testing frameworks
Broad polyglot engineering range (D, TypeScript/Node.js, Java, Swift) with a consistent testing and software-craftsmanship discipline
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++; C++ is not even listed among his current skills
No experience building or operating a cloud compute platform at scale; infrastructure exposure is deploy-and-consume rather than platform ownership
No formal technical-lead role leading technical strategy, aligning teams, or managing project priorities and deadlines
